scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented WorkflowMax API v2 endpoint is enumerated below. Legacy's own base URL (api.workflowmax2.com) matches the real v2 host, but legacy's paths (/jobs, /clients, /contacts with no account-id header) do not match any real v2 endpoint (v2 requires /v2/<resource> paths plus a required account-id header, and v2 has no bare GET /contacts list endpoint at all -- client-contacts only support get-by-id/create/update/delete, never a list). This bundle corrects the paths/headers to the real, working v2 surface (jobs, clients, client-contacts) while keeping the same 3 legacy-parity resource families; every other v2 resource family (staff, tasks, costs, custom fields, custom rates, leads, quotes, quote-variations, invoices, purchase orders, suppliers, supplier-contacts, timesheets, capacity-plan, business-structure/client-type reference data, roles, account/me) is out of scope for this migration and excluded below.

excluded.reason: detail-by-id is fully covered by the jobs list stream's records; no fan-out justification for a second per-record request
job update accepts a large partial-update surface (status/category/priority/staffAssigned/customFields transitions) not modeled by this Pass B pass; create/delete are covered, update is deferred
job-template instantiation is a compound/templated creation flow, not a plain resource write
reference/lookup data for job creation, not a synced business-data resource

client document upload is a binary file transfer flow, not an expressible dialect write

get-by-id is the read side of update_client_contact's own target resource; there is no bare list endpoint for client-contacts at all (v2 has no GET /v2/clients/contacts collection route), so this resource is exposed only through its write actions rather than a synced stream -- see docs.md Known limits
alternate create-contact-under-client shape; POST /v2/clients/contacts (covered) is the canonical create used by this bundle

alternate delete-contact-under-client shape; DELETE /v2/clients/contacts/{uuid} (covered) is the canonical delete used by this bundle
